# Azion Web Platform Documentation

> Documentation for the Azion Web Platform: build, secure, deploy and observe
> applications on a global distributed network. Bilingual (en, pt-br); every URL
> below has a pt-br twin under /pt-br/documentacao/.

## Important notes for AI agents

- REST API base: `https://api.azion.com/v4`. Authentication header: `Authorization: Token [TOKEN]`.
- Use the current product names. Renamed in 2024–2025: Applications (formerly Edge Application),
  Functions (Edge Functions), Cache (Edge Cache), Firewall (Edge Firewall), Object Storage
  (Edge Storage), SQL Database (Edge SQL), KV Store (Edge KV), Orchestrator (Edge Orchestrator),
  Certificate Manager (Digital Certificates), Network Shield (Network Layer Protection),
  Data Stream (Data Streaming). Edge DNS, Edge Pulse, Edge Node and Edge Services keep the prefix.
- v4 concepts: use **Connectors** (formerly Origins) and **Workloads** (formerly Domains).
  Never recommend Origins or Domains — they do not exist in the v4 API.
- MCP server: https://mcp.azion.com — documentation, code samples and CLI command search tools.

## Get started

- [Get started with Azion](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/): Start here: deploy your first application on Azion in minutes using a template, a GitHub repository, or the Azion CLI, and find the right path for your goal.
- [First deploy in 5 minutes](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/first-deploy/): Deploy your first application on Azion using a template, a GitHub repository, or the Azion CLI, and verify it's live with an HTTP 200 response on your Azion domain.
- [How to migrate your application to Azion](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/migrate/): Learn how you can bring your applications and infrastructure to Azion.
- [Go live with Azion](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/production-checklist/): This documentation will guide you to add a custom domain and point your traffic to Azion's network.
- [Start with a template](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/start-with-a-template/): This documentation will assist you to build a new application on Azion using templates.
- [Hosting an Edge Site](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/first-deploy/cli/): Azion's Edge Computing platform has serverless functions that enable Edge Websites to be created and all processing done directly on our Edge Nodes, without having to access any origin infrastructure.
- [Use a template via Azion Console](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/first-deploy/template/): Simplify and optimize your workflow with templates on Azion Console. Enhance productivity and make operations effortlessly, saving time with existing templates.
- [How to build with Angular](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/frameworks/angular/): Learn to initialize and deploy your Angular project on the Azion Web Platform using Functions, enhancing your web development capabilities.
- [How to build with Astro](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/frameworks/astro/): Learn how to initialize and deploy an Astro project on the Azion platform using Functions, featuring guides on setting up with the Azion CLI and managing deployments.
- [Develop with Azion CLI](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/frameworks/cli/): Azion's CLI (command-line interface) is an open source tool that enables the management of services offered by Azion.
- [Functions Code Editor Overview](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/frameworks/code-editor/): The code editor for Functions is a good fit to get started developing at the edge.
- [How to build with Docusaurus](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/frameworks/docusaurus/): Learn how to initialize and deploy an Docusaurus project on the Azion platform using Functions, featuring guides on setting up with the Azion CLI and managing deployments.
- [How to build with Eleventy](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/frameworks/eleventy/): Learn how to initialize and deploy an Eleventy project on the Azion platform using Functions, featuring guides on setting up with the Azion CLI and managing deployments.
- [Build with Environment Variables](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/frameworks/environment-variables/): Explore how Environment Variables enhance security and flexibility in software development using Functions through the Azion API.
- [How to build with Gatsby](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/frameworks/gatsby/): Learn how to initialize and deploy an Gatsby project on the Azion platform using Functions, featuring guides on setting up with the Azion CLI and managing deployments.
- [Build with Azion Go SDK](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/frameworks/go/): The Azion SDK for Go facilitates the use of the Azion APIs by providing a set of tools that make the development of Go applications smoother.
- [How to build with Hexo](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/frameworks/hexo/): Learn to deploy a Hexo project using the Azion CLI to the Edge Platform, covering setup, local development, and Functions integration.
- [How to build with Hono](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/frameworks/hono/): Learn how to initialize and deploy an Hono project on the Azion platform using Functions, featuring guides on setting up with the Azion CLI and managing deployments.
- [How to build with Hugo](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/frameworks/hugo/): Learn how to initialize and deploy an Hugo project on the Azion platform using Functions, featuring guides on setting up with the Azion CLI and managing deployments.
- [How to build functions](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/frameworks/javascript/): Explore how to develop and debug serverless Functions using JavaScript on the Azion Runtime, enhancing your Applications.
- [How to build with Jekyll](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/frameworks/jekyll/): Learn how to initialize and deploy an Jekyll project on the Azion platform using Functions, featuring guides on setting up with the Azion CLI and managing deployments.
- [Local development](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/frameworks/local-dev/): Explore how Azion CLI enhances local development for Applications with efficient debugging, accelerated workflows, and collaborative features.
- [How to build with Next.js](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/frameworks/next/): Learn how to initialize and deploy a Next.js project on the Azion Edge Platform, leveraging Functions and the latest Node.js features.
- [How to build with Nextal](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/frameworks/nextal/): Learn how to initialize and deploy a Nextal project on the Azion Web Platform using the OpenNextJS preset and Azion CLI, combining Next.js, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS.
- [Develop with Azion Overview](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/frameworks/overview/): Explore the revolution of Jamstack architecture with Edge Computing, using tools like Azion Bundler and Azion CLI for efficient web app development on the Azion platform.
- [How to build with React](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/frameworks/react/): Learn how to deploy a React project using Azion CLI, optimize it for the Edge, and utilize Functions, with a complete guide on setting up and deploying on the Azion platform.
- [Build with Azion Runtime](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/frameworks/runtime-apis/): Check out the documentation for the JavaScript Runtime Environment on Azion Edge Platform.
- [How to build with Svelte](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/frameworks/svelte/): Learn how to initialize and deploy an Svelte project on the Azion platform using Functions, featuring guides on setting up with the Azion CLI and managing deployments.
- [Build with Azion Terraform Provider](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/frameworks/terraform-provider/): Explore the Azion Terraform Provider, an open source tool for managing infrastructures on the Azion platform with Terraform, using the Azion SDK for efficient integration.
- [How to build with VitePress](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/frameworks/vite/): Learn how to initialize and deploy a VitePress project on the Azion Web Platform using Functions and the Azion CLI, enhancing your frontend development skills.
- [How to build with Vue](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/frameworks/vue/): Explore Vue.js for building seamless web interfaces with a guide on initializing and deploying your project using Azion Functions and Platform.
- [How to build with WebAssembly](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/frameworks/wasm/): Explore how to create Functions using JavaScript and WebAssembly on Azion, including guide on wasmCode implementation for Edge Computing.
- [Build an application](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/journeys/launch/): Explore how to deploy and configure your Applications with Azion's comprehensive tools, enhancing security, automation, and performance.
- [Secure an application](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/journeys/protect/): Use Firewall to secure your applications.
- [Migrate from Akamai to Azion | Full Guide](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/migrate/akamai/): Learn how to migrate from Akamai to Azion with less operational risk. Migrate Akamai Ion, App & API Protector, EdgeWorkers, EdgeKV, Cloudlets, Edge DNS, Global Traffic Management, NetStorage, DataStream, mPulse, Prolexic, and related delivery, compute, security, storage, DNS, and observability workflows to Azion Applications, Functions, KV Store, Object Storage, Firewall, Edge DNS, Load Balancer, Data Stream, Edge Pulse, Real-Time Events, and Real-Time Metrics.
- [Migrate from AWS to Azion | Full Guide](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/migrate/aws/): Learn how to migrate from AWS to Azion with less operational risk. Migrate CloudFront, Lambda, S3, DynamoDB, Route 53, WAF, and other AWS services to Azion Applications, Functions, Object Storage, KV Store, Edge DNS, and security products. Azion provides a complete migration path from AWS compute, storage, database, security, and observability services to a unified edge platform.
- [Migrate from Cloudflare to Azion | Full Guide](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/migrate/cloudflare/): Learn how to migrate from Cloudflare to Azion with less operational risk. Preserve domains, redirects, environment variables, Functions, KV data, Object Storage, SQL Database, security rules, and observability workflows. Azion provides a complete migration path from Cloudflare Pages, Workers, Workers KV, R2, D1, and configuration features to Azion Applications, Functions, KV Store, Object Storage, and SQL Database. The platform combines build, compute, storage, security, and observability in a unified environment designed for globally distributed applications.
- [Migrate from Fastly to Azion | Full Guide](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/migrate/fastly/): Learn how to migrate from Fastly to Azion with less operational risk. Migrate Fastly Full-Site Delivery, Compute, Edge Data Storage, Object Storage, Image Optimizer, WAF, DDoS protection, bot controls, TLS, logging, and observability workflows to Azion Applications, Functions, KV Store, Object Storage, Image Processor, Firewall, Certificate Manager, Data Stream, Real-Time Events, and Real-Time Metrics.
- [Migrate from Vercel to Azion | Complete migration guide for modern applications](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/migrate/vercel/): Learn how to migrate from Vercel to Azion with less operational risk. Migrate Vercel projects, production deployments, preview deployments, CDN configuration, Functions, Fluid Compute, AI integrations, Blob, Edge Config, Firewall, WAF, bot controls, domains, DNS, analytics, and observability workflows to Azion Applications, Functions, AI Inference, Object Storage, KV Store, Firewall, Web Application Firewall, Edge DNS, Certificate Manager, Real-Time Metrics, Real-Time Events, Data Stream, and Edge Pulse.
- [How to manage the Azion GitHub App](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/first-deploy/github/azion-github-app/): This documentation explains how the Azion GitHub App works and how to manage it according to your preferences.
- [Import & Deploy a Project from GitHub](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/first-deploy/github/import-an-existing-project-from-github/): This documentation assists you in importing your own GitHub repository to build and deploy an application on Azion's edge.
- [Secure DNS](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/journeys/protect/dns/): Discover how to use Azion Secure products for your DNS. Strengthen your online security with robust DNS protection for your digital assets with Edge DNS.
- [Secure an infrastructure](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/get-started/journeys/protect/infrastructure/): Use Origin Shield to secure your infrastructure.

## Platform

- [Azion Network Program](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/platform/network-program/): Founded in 2011, Azion enables you to build and run serverless applications at the edge of the network or your own premisses, closer to users and devices.
- [Azion Web Platform Overview](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/platform/overview/): This documentation introduces the Azion Web Platform, which unifies app development, security, and observability on a global infrastructure so you can ship faster with performance and control.
- [Pricing](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/platform/pricing/): Check prices for Azion products, services, and Support.
- [Shared Responsibility Model](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/platform/shared-responsibility/): This document highlights the roles and responsibilities of each party when organizations use the Azion Platform.
- Full section index: https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/platform/llms.txt (7 pages)

## Build

- [Explore Azion Build products](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/): Build and run your application with Cache, Application Accelerator, and Functions right on Azion's global Edge Computing network.
- [AI Inference](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/ai-inference/): Azion AI Inference empowers you to build and deploy intelligent applications that process data close to where it is generated.
- [Applications](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/): Applications is a product that lets you build applications on Azion Global Network, reducing latency and throughput between origin servers and users.
- [Connectors](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/connectors/): Connectors allows you to connect your origin to an Application. An origin represents the server from which Azion retrieves your content, such as an object storage service or a cloud server.
- [Overview](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/overview/): Build applications closer to end-users, ensuring low-latency processing at the edge.
- [Workloads](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/workloads/): Workloads is an Azion Console tool that centralizes communication protocol settings, such as certificates, domains, ports and protocols.
- Full section index: https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/llms.txt (171 pages)

## Store

- [Explore Azion Store products](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/store/): Azion Store products let you store, query, and manage data close to your users and applications.
- [KV Store](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/store/kv-store/): KV Store is a distributed key-value storage service that runs across Azion's network, providing low-latency data persistence and retrieval.
- [Object Storage](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/store/object-storage/): Azion Object Storage is a scalable and secure storage service designed to integrate object storage with the Azion Web Platform using the S3 standard.
- [Store Overview](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/store/overview/): Explore Azion SQL Database and Storage for scalable data management.
- [SQL Database](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/store/sql-database/): Explore SQL Database, a distributed database solution by Azion. It allows you to manage databases, run queries, and ensure data resilience effortlessly.
- Full section index: https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/store/llms.txt (38 pages)

## Secure

- [Explore Azion Secure products](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/): Azion's Secure products guarantee the security, availability, and authenticity of your application's data.
- [Azion Bot Manager](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/bot-manager/): Protect your applications against automated attacks such as bad bot signatures or scripted bots.
- [Certificate Manager](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/certificate-manager/): Certificate Manager is the Secure product where you register and manage the TLS certificates that enable HTTPS traffic for your domains at Azion.
- [Custom Pages](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/custom-pages/): Custom Pages is a functionality that allows you to customize error pages for users based on the status code received from the edge connectors.
- [DDoS Protection](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/ddos-protection/): Azion DDoS Protection defends your content and applications against Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks.
- [Edge DNS](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/edge-dns/): Edge DNS is an authoritative DNS service that hosts your domains, zones, and records on Azion's distributed infrastructure, with DDoS protection and real-time record distribution.
- [Firewall](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/firewall/): Azion Firewall is a security product that protects from the network layer to the application layer.
- [Network Shield](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/network-shield/): Explore Azion's Network Shield for robust firewall security. Safeguard your infrastructure with granular control and advanced filtering.
- [Secure Overview](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/overview/): Secure your applications and domains with edge computing protection.
- [Web Application Firewall](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/waf/): Protect your applications against threats like SQL Injections, Remote File Inclusion (RFI), Cross-Site Scripting (XSS), and others with Azion Web Application Firewall.
- Full section index: https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/llms.txt (66 pages)

## Deploy

- [Explore Azion Deploy products](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/deploy/): Run your applications anywhere: on Azion’s highly distributed network, on multi-cloud, or on-premises.
- [Orchestrator](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/deploy/orchestrator/): Azion Orchestrator is an end-to-end encrypted orchestration service with cloud management and zero-touch provisioning, created for large-scale Edge networks. Manage and control resources on the edge in real time and orchestrate your services quickly and easily to meet your needs.
- [Deploy Overview](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/deploy/overview/): Azion Deploy products are designed to enhance the efficiency of managing edge resources in real time.
- Full section index: https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/deploy/llms.txt (21 pages)

## Observe

- [Explore Azion Observe products](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/observe/): Azion Observe products assist you in monitoring and accessing information related to your data.
- [Data Stream](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/observe/data-stream/): Data Stream is an Observe product that allows you to feed your SIEM, big data, and stream processing platforms with access to your content and applications data in real time, adding even more intelligence to your business.
- [Edge Pulse](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/observe/edge-pulse/): Discover how to get detailed insights into your customers' experience and improve your content delivery with Edge Pulse.
- [Observe Overview](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/observe/overview/): Observe your application's data and improve your decision-making process.
- [Real-Time Events](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/observe/real-time-events/): Real-Time Events is an Observe product that allows you to display logs from your Azion products and services in real time.
- [Real-Time Metrics](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/observe/real-time-metrics/): Real-Time Metrics provides you with real-time access to metrics and helps you optimize the use of Azion products and how your content is delivered.
- [SIEM Azion](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/observe/siem/): Analyze data from dashboards with raw and aggregated data.
- Full section index: https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/observe/llms.txt (51 pages)

## Dev Tools

- [Dev Tools](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/): Discover tools that help you take your applications one step further and find more information such as reference documentation, guides, and tutorials. These developer tools — including the API, GraphQL API, Azion Lib, CLI, SDKs, the Terraform Provider, and the MCP server — help you build, automate, and manage your Azion applications end to end, from local development to production.
- [Get started with Azion API](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/api/): Learn more about official endpoints, methods, and API use cases of the Azion RESTful API.
- [Azion CLI](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/cli/): Explore Azion CLI for edge computing manage and deploy applications, integrate with Azion APIs, and optimize with Go SDK.
- [Console Kit](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/console-kit/): Developed with Vue/Vite, PrimeVue, and Tailwind, Azion Console Kit is a front-end development kit used for crafting a customized Azion Console interface.
- [For AI Agents](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/for-ai-agents/): Entry point for AI agents and their developers building on Azion: canonical facts, current product names, API base URL, auth format, and machine-readable resources such as llms.txt and the Azion MCP server.
- [Form Builder](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/form-builder/): Build visual, schema-driven forms to configure Azion Functions arguments in Applications and Firewalls.
- [GraphQL API](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/graphql/): Request the data you want and receive nothing more, nothing less. The Azion GraphQL API lets you query metrics, events, and accounting data with precise, flexible requests, retrieving exactly the fields you need in a single call instead of over-fetching or under-fetching information.
- [Azion Libraries overview](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/lib/): Learn about Azion Libraries, a suite of tools for interacting with Azion services, products, and utilities.
- [Model Context Protocol (MCP)](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/mcp/): Connect AI code assistants to Azion Platform through the Model Context Protocol, enabling intelligent assistance for deployment, configuration, and management tasks.
- [Azion Terraform Provider](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/terraform/): Azion Terraform Provider v2.0 is an open source project that enables infrastructure as code management for Azion API v4. Manage workloads, connectors, applications, and more through Terraform configurations.
- Full section index: https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/llms.txt (101 pages)

## Marketplace

- Full section index: https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/marketplace/llms.txt (137 pages)

## Account

- [Explore Azion Accounts](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/account/): Manage your account, user permissions, teams, Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA), Single Sign-On (SSO), and subscribe to Azion products.
- Full section index: https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/account/llms.txt (37 pages)

## Architectures

- [Solutions](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/architectures/): Azion solutions reflect how components interact and operate within the platform to attend to different use cases.
- Full section index: https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/architectures/llms.txt (28 pages)

## Services

- [Best Practices Review](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/services/best-practices-review/): Offers a thorough evaluation of your infrastructure and provides expert recommendations to optimize performance, security, and efficiency.
- [Business Events Support](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/services/business-events-support/): Business Events Support refers to the support and assistance provided to assist customers during events, in real time.
- [Instructor-Led Training](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/services/instructor-led-training/): Instructor-Led Training offers learning experiences adapted to your specific needs.
- [Integration Services](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/services/integration-services/): The fastest and safest way to migrate your content, web applications, and APIs to Azion, no matter how complex and resource-intensive your infrastructure is.
- [Managed Configurations](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/services/managed-configurations/): Managed Configurations employs the expertise of a team of experienced engineers to tackle certain day-to-day tasks on your behalf, such as configuring and maintaining your configurations.
- [Security Response Team](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/services/security-response-team/): Managed service that authorizes Azion to impose technical measures available on the Azion's platform to control or mitigate DDoS attacks, even if such measures may result in an eventual performance degradation.
- [Slack Channel](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/services/slack-channel/): The Slack Channel service refers to support and assistance provided to assist customers through written communication.
- [Technical Support](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/services/support/): At Azion, you're served by a team of engineers who are specialized in performance, security, high availability, and scalability.
- [Technical Account Manager](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/services/technical-account-manager/): The TAM acts as a consultant, proactively providing strategic technical guidance to help accelerate the digital transformation and edge computing journey, and provide insights to maximize infrastructure investment
- Full section index: https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/services/llms.txt (12 pages)

## Changelog

- [Release notes](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/changelog/): Check the latest changes and release notes in the Azion Web Platform, Azion API, CLI, and other channels and products.
- [Changelog previous years](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/changelog/archive/): Explore major updates for Azion's products and services, including Edge Applications, Data Stream, Edge Firewall adjustments and API V3 developments.
- Full section index: https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/changelog/llms.txt (2 pages)
